C[signius: When white makes this sort of move to cut black, expect black to cut in response. Unless looking to start a fight (which is not necessarily bad) avoid pushing black too hard.
C[signius: This move is ok to reinforce the link between g4 and k4, but bigger moves elsewhere. Consider c11 to invade or c4 to reinforce the corner
C[signius: c4 has a mich higher probability of living - d11b then c10w, and vice versa.

C[DanielBR: Not good move. Black should use a star point (Q4) joseki, R6 is a good choice.
C[DanielBR: Another mistake move. If black want protect the top, then F17 or O17 are better place for make a good balance between 4 and 3 line.
C[DanielBR: I used the basic strategy on handcap games trying to split all start point stones (4-4)
C[DanielBR: After many moves, this is the first time white need respond black move. But black will get the influence at the middle, then he have to know how exploit this.
C[DanielBR: The safe move would be R14
C[DanielBR: Black make a wall on Q16, but instead play on R7, should extend, protect and use this wall (R13 or R12 do this)

C[DanielBR: Trying protect all stones, will make you lose more of these. Less group => less stones to protect and more free to attack. And because this, white will try split back in many groups. Then black will sacrifice some isolated stones or risk lose a lot.
C[DanielBR: Instead of B13, black should fight the KO using O9 or S14 (because black haven't nothing to lose making this)
C[DanielBR: Instead of attack here, black should protect the C12 stone and get a large territory on the left. The D12 would be a good choice.
C[rjcchan: good idea. thanx for playing

GN[Teaching game for 24kyu and weaker only please]
C[VipassanaMan: Here you have given up the corner - don't do that. Corners are too valuable - corners first, then sides.
C[VipassanaMan: Your D4 stone here is now very weak - only two liberties. Here you should have strengthened it with D5 instead.
C[VipassanaMan: Trying to keep me trapped in the corner is fine - but this move gains you little territory. D5 is still urgent, but otherwise play elsewhere (tenuki) e.g. N3 or N4 etc.
C[VipassanaMan: You should have played this move before me :)
C[rjcchan: it seems to me that i should be focusing elsewhere but i hate peep holes.  Would it have been better to seal in the corner or start protecting the rest of the board?

C[VipassanaMan: When your peephole gets attacked it normally results in a ladder. If I have ladder breakers in place then you closing it might be the right move. In this case there are none, so you could (should) have saved the move.
C[VipassanaMan: Here H2 would have been better - the aim is to prevent me connecting my corner to the stranded stones at K3 etc.
C[VipassanaMan: Here you could still have cut me with J2... me J3 you G2 etc
C[VipassanaMan: N5 better to close the gap you have left for me.

C[VipassanaMan: Be careful here.... If you attack and the attack fails, you have just helped me strengthen my position. This is something weaker players do a lot so watch out for it. Try never to do moves that don't benefit you but make them stronger instead.
VipassanaMan: Moves like this are bad because they give me a free chance to strengthen my own defences. Don't play them unless you see an opportunity to attack successfully.
C[rjcchan: it feels more like defending on three fronts than attacking

C[VipassanaMan: I know the feeling! Remember you don't need to annihilate me.... just end the game with more territory. You have to give up some. Being too aggressive normally leads to a bigger loss when your attacks fail.
C[VipassanaMan: Probably I should have attacked where your stone is rather than the other side.
C[rjcchan: i have read that you should not push from behind - but is there a choice here?
C[VipassanaMan: Actually here it would be me pushing from behind, if i were to play J16. Your move was correct (to avoid a monkey jump).
C[VipassanaMan: You might find this helpful:
C[rjcchan: there is a lot of material there. thanx.
C[rjcchan: there must be a better move here than this
C[VipassanaMan: Here I should probably have been thinking about a new attack on the left side 2 line or 3 line and see if i could get away with it.... but already probably too late for it.
C[VipassanaMan: I think this move by me was a bad one - I should have attacked (as I was behind), rather than conceding the status quo.
C[rjcchan: Thank you for playing and for your comments
C[Thyderion: You don't have enough space to make two eyes inside my territory without me making a huge mistake.
C[rjcchan: might as well get thrashed again
C[rjcchan: might as well get thrashed again
C[cook777: When someone attaches to a stone like this (and this early in the game) you normally dont play away from it.
C[cook777: Oooops I miss read what you said. I read thrash as might as well we thrash again. (Thrash used to mean fight) sorry
C[cook777: here I was worried I made a mistake somere that cost me the game. D4 stone is weak with many black stones around it. No eyes (or eye) here.
C[cook777: I feel like looking at this side of the board makes my head spin.
C[cook777: Hi again and have a good game.
C[cook777: Hi again and have a good game.
C[cook777: The W group has no eyes. Just a string of White stones serrounded by Black stones.
C[cook777: made no eyes for W and didn't really attack my group.

C[rjcchan: A2 instead of b3  would have given you the corner.

C[ObieTwice: Hello. Good luck. I hope you enjoy the game.
C[ObieTwice: Hello. Good luck. I hope you enjoy the game.
C[ObieTwice: Based on what we now know as the final score, black probably wins by keeping white out of the middle here.
ObieTwice: Black playing P11 rather than P10 may result in larger life on the right for white but will keep white out of the middle.
rjcchan: Allowing white a large territory on the right is what i was worried about.
C[ObieTwice: I think black's move was a bit too locally focused. The main goal here should probably be to prevent white from connecting PR14 stones with R11 group. Maybe Q13 is better here than Q14.
C[ObieTwice: I think white was over-extended here and could have been cut without this protection. Black should consider taking the reward at T16-17.
rjcchan: It is called hitting the submit button while your brain is saying ""wait a minute""
ObieTwice: Lol. I do that way too much!
ObieTwice: A lot of mistakes made online would never be made in person.
C[ObieTwice: Nice recognition. Letting white invade at P19 would have been disastrous.
C[ObieTwice: There are ""bigger moves"" on the board. Without this move, any later push by white can be stopped at L13. Here, I would push at F18 then J16.

C[ObieTwice: i noticed this move late... if black plays at O3 first, consider o2-P2, q2-N2, p1-L1 to put M3 group in atari and capture. intermediate players will recognize this and initally play N2 as a response rather than Q2 but those ""second atari threat"" combos are always worth considering.

ObieTwice: i lost a match at go club the other day because of this... was up 12 and lost by 4. (not considering komi or handicap)... but i was playing a 6kyu with a 4 stone advantage. So frustrating!!
rjcchan: did see this earlier but figured you would block with N2
ObieTwice: snapbacks are a pain. recognizing the setup for them is a must against some players.
C[rjcchan: think i'll play the whiskey joseki on myself ....:)
C[ObieTwice: in the true spirit of joseki, everyone wins
C[ObieTwice: This is one of those goofy double snapbacks. It is probably not worth dealing with while other points are not claimed.
rjcchan: At this point it was over anyways so  i was just crossing theee t's
